AUTHORIZATION OF REPRESENTATIVE TO APPEAL

| understand that Medicare and other payers do not pay the difference in the cost between a private and semi-private room when use of a private room is not necessary for medical reasons. | hereby agree that the difference in the cost between a semi-private and private room will be my responsibility to pay if | request one at any point during my stay.

| hereby authorize and convey to UofL Health the full extent permissible under Federal and State law and under any applicable insurance policy and/or employee health care benefit plan: (1) the right and ability to act as my Authorized Representative in connection with any claim, right, matter, or cause of action that | may have under such insurance policy and/or benefit plan; and (2) the right and ability to act as my Authorized Representative to pursue such claim, right, matter, or cause of action in connection with said insurance policy and/or benefit plan, including the right and ability to act as my Authorized Representative with respect to a benefit plan governed by the provisions of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act ("ERISA").

| also authorize UofL Health to act on my behalf to (1) obtain copies of my insurance policy or benefit plan documents from my insurer(s) or other Plan Administrator; (2) pursue appeals of adverse benefits decisions, claims for breaches of fiduciary duty under ERISA, external appeal review under Federal or State statute, administrative hearings, or legal actions to obtain payment from any payor; (3) disclose my protected health information to my insurer(s), its affiliates, any plan fiduciary, or independent review organization that may be involved in the resolution of claims, in connection with filing or appealing any claim for benefits due; and (4) receive any notices in connection with my benefit claims or appeals concerning same.

If my admission to UofL Health is a result of a work-related injury, | hereby waive any privilege | may have with UofL Health, or other healthcare provider, and | hereby authorize these providers to provide the worker's compensation administrator any information related to my injury or to my past relevant medical history. In the event there is a dispute about the compensability of my claim or worker's compensation benefits, and if my employer is not specifically determined by a Court of the Department of Labor to be responsible for worker's compensation medical expenses for the condition or injury that is the basis of my admission, | agree to be personally responsible for all such expenses. | further agree that, if my worker's compensation claim is settled with my employer on a disputed basis without a specific finding that such is compensable as a worker's compensation injury, | (or my attorney, if | am represented) will withhold sufficient funds from any settlement to pay all amounts owed to UofL Health for treatment of the condition which is the basis for this admission, and | hereby grant an assignment to UofL Health for payment of all such expenses under such circumstances.

PERSONAL EQUIPMENT AND VALUABLES

| understand that UofL Health does not accept responsibility and will not reimburse me for the loss of money, jewelry, eyewear, hearing aids, dentures, clothing or other personal property or valuables | bring to UofL Health. | take full responsibility for such items and agree to release UofL Health from any and all liability from damage, loss or theft of such items unless, as applicable, the items are deposited at UofL Health safe for safekeeping. | also understand that | must inform a nurse if | bring any electrical equipment to UofL Health (e.g., home insulin pumps; BiPAP machine, CPAP machine) and adhere to UoflL Health policies regarding its use. | assume full responsibility for such electrical equipment and for any injury caused by the use of the electrical equipment brought from home.

COMMUNICATIONS

By providing my cell, landline, or any other phone number(s), | expressly consent to receiving communications from UofL Health, its staff, its contractors, collection agents, and others, at any numbers | provide or that are later acquired for me. These parties may use this information to contact me by live agent, voice mail, text message, using an auto dialer or other computer assisted technology, pre-recorded message(s), or by any other form of electronic communication for any purpose including, but not limited to, appointment and follow-up health care reminders, scheduling, my account(s), assignment of benefits, and/or financial responsibility. | understand that depending on my phone plan | could be charged for these calls or text messages. | agree to provide new number(s) if my number(s) change(s). Providing these numbers is not a condition of receiving healthcare services.

USING AND SHARING MY INFORMATION

The Law: There is a federal law called the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (“HIPAA”). This law requires UofL Health to protect the privacy and security of its patients’ treatment, contact, and financial information. Taken together, this information is called your “Health Information”. There are also other federal and/or state laws that require UofL Health to take additional steps to protect certain hie of Health Information, including, but not limited to, Health Information about behavioral or mental health; developmental disabilities; treatment for substance abuse (alcohol and/or drugs) disorders; genetic testing and counseling; HIV/AIDS; sexual assault) abuse; sexually transmitted illnesses; pregnancy; birth control; domestic abuse of an adult with a disability; child abuse and neglect.

Authorization and Notice of Privacy Practices (“NPP”):If my permission is required by law, by signing this form | agree that UofL Health may receive, use and disclose my Health Information as set forth in this rt bn and as set forth in UofL Health's NPP. | understand that | can find more information about my rights to my Health Information, and about how UofL Health uses my Health Information, in tte NPP. | acknowledge that if | requested a copy of the NPP that | have been given a copy. | further understand that the NPP is available on UofL Health’s website at: www.uoflhealth.org.

| agree that my permission applies to all of my Health Information in UofL Health’s possession, including but not limited to my contact information, problem list, diagnostic test results, medication list, allergy list, immunizations, medical history, surgeries, procedures, and other clinically relevant data.

| understand that UofL Health cannot control how others that receive my Health Information will protect or use my information. | understand that others may not be required by law to protect my Health Information.

Purposes for which my Health Information may be shared: | understand that UofL Health may receive, use, and disclose my Health Information for the purposes outlined in the NPP. In particular, UofL Health may use and disclose my Health Information for the following purposes:

Data Sharing Program Purposes: | understand and agree that the use and disclosure of my health information for certain aspects of treatment at UofL Health may be disclosed to other people involved with my care outside UofL Health. If | desire to revoke my consent, even in an emergency situation, | must notify UofL Health’s Health Information Management Department by phone: [PHONE-2334], option 2. | understand and agree that my request will be evaluated to determine if it is reasonable and in the best interest of my health care to comply with my request. | will be notified in writing of as to whether UofL Health will be able to grant my request. If my request is granted and | am are in need of emergency treatment, Uofl Health may end the restriction if the information is needed to provide my care. (See our Notice of Privacy Practices for more information on these types of uses and disclosures for further information.)

Fundraising Activities: Uofl. Health relies on fundraising to help support caring for all patients. | give my permission to be contacted relating to fundraising opportunities. If | prefer not to be contacted about fundraising, | must notify UofL Health by email to: [EMAIL-2335] or by phone: [PHONE-2336]

Operational Purposes: | agree that the contact information | give to UofL Health, such as telephone numbers and email addresses, may be used by UofL Health and third parties acting for UoflL Health to communicate with me for operational purposes including appointment reminders, follow up treatment reminders, flu shot reminders, and patient surveys.

Treatment Note Assessment : Pt. is progressing very well with her mobility and on this date was able to perform all bed mobility, transfers and ambulation without assist, no safety concerns noted. Her activity is limited by R ankle pain but she is very motivated as she wants to return home with family. At this time patient is mobilizing well enough to return home but if she remains in the hospital will follow up 1-2 more times. Additional Objective Information : Gait Training: Pt. was supine in bed upon entry with B CAM boots donned. She transitioned into sitting independently and with walker in place she stood without assist. She ambulated approx. 80 feet with decreased cadence 2/2 pain but no unsteadiness or LOB noted and no assist required from therapist. She was provided initial cues for maintaining walker at appropriate distance as she tended to step to closely and during activity therapist adjusted walker for more proper fit. Pt. returned to her room and transferred stand to sit on EOB independently. Self-care/Home Management: PT reviewed proper technique for donning/doffing CAM boot and she demonstrated understanding. Pt. had questions regarding follow up PT after discharge and other potential DME needed for the home. All questions were answered and both patient and significant other voiced understanding.

Treatment Note

Subjective Comment: Pt pleasant and agreeable to working with therapy, wanting to go to BR. CAM boots already in place. Additional Objective Information : Pt sup>sit with Ind. Pt ed'd on WBAT to B LE's and FWW retrieved and sized to pt. Pt needing verbal cues to push up from bed with single UE and one on FWW, she was able to complete with Touch A. Pt ed'd on mgt of FWW and step length to prevent posterior LOB she was able to make corrections. She amb'd x 8' x 2 w/ Touch A. Pt with expected gait impairments due to CAM boot limiting ROM at ankle. Discussed up/down ramp with step to pattern and use of gait belt to enter the home. Pt also ed'd on lifestyle modification while healing as she expects to return to work as cashier standing on feet and still going out socializing with friends. Assessment : Pt doing well with mobility post-op. She has pain in WBing as to be expected and impaired gait mechanics due to CAM boots. Will continue to follow while here to optimize Ind and safety with all mobility.

Pertinent laboratory results, radiographic and advanced Imaging reports reviewed. Pertinent radiographs and advanced imaging also viewed and reviewed by me personally. Agree with above with

the following additions/changes:

time of injury.

Ras:

Constitutional: No fevers or chills

ENT: No hearing problems

Respiratory; No shortness of breath

Gastrointestinal; No abdominal pain

Musculoskeletal: + bilateral ankle pain

Skin: + scrapes and bruises

[DOB-2826] = 10:47 GOULDS DISCOUNT MEDICAL (FAX)[PHONE-2827] P.008/012

Hand P DOWNS, JUANITA NECOLE « [PHONE-2828] * Final Report *

Exam:

Vitals as above

Cardiovascular: Bilateral radial and PT pulses palpable

Lymphatic: None palpable about, te extremities

Skin: Visible skin intact in the RUE, LUE, RLE, LLE

Neuro: Coordination intact, Patellar tendon reflexes not tested due to injury. Sensation intact to light tough RUE, LUE, RLE, LLE, normal mood and affect, alert and oriented to person, place, time and events.

Constitutional: Well developed, well nourished, Vitals as above

MSK:

As above

No signs of DVT, PE, or compartment syndrome.

AP:

1, Right medial malleolus fracture: will require surgical stabilization to restore fength, aligament, and function, Will require postaperative pain control and physical therapy. Discussed increased risk of PTOA due to intra-articular nature of the fracture,

2, Left tibiotalar dislocation with small avulsion fractures of the talus: will require EUA of the ankle stability. If unstable,

external fixation will be required; however, if stable, amenable to nonoperative management in a CAM boot, the avulsion fractures require no surgical intervention. Discussed increased risk of PTOA due to distocation and cartilaginous injury along with AVN.

2, Risks, benefits and alternatives of operative vs non-operative treatment discussed extensively, Specific risks discussed induded but were not limited bo; blood loss requiring transfusion, infection/ostcomyelitls passibly requiring multiple future surgeries, malunion/nonunion, the possibility of future surgerie¢s, chronic pain, disability, damage to nerves, tendons or blood vessels, DVT/PE, and death, Ail questions answered. No guarantees given,

4, Tobacoo use: discussed the adverse effects of continued nicotine exposure while healing wounds and fractures, specifically the increased risk of nonunion, Infection, and wound complications. Strongly recommend cessation.

As patient ate only hours before surgery, will delay until 4/18 AM,
